title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, of Marston Mills, was charged with aggravated battery after allegedly stabbing his friend at a Miami Beach party.
The altercation reportedly began over attention from women at bars, leading to a confrontation in an SUV on Ocean Drive.
Both Dickerson and the victim, Brian Gordon, sustained injuries; Dickerson remains in treatment, and Gordon was released from the hospital.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, marking their third defeat in four 2026 matches.
Right back Ilay Feingold scored early for New England, but St. Louis responded with three unanswered goals.
Forward Leo Campana left the game with a new injury, unrelated to his previous condition, after entering as a sub.

title: Don’t be fooled by this fake court notice scam in Mass., NH, authorities warn
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Authorities warn of fake court notices with errors in Massachusetts and New Hampshire.
Scam notices falsely claim traffic violations, urge QR code payments, and feature errors like misspelled 'Massachuseetts'.
Recipients advised to delete the notices and verify court information through official channels.

title: The trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons begins this week. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover police officer, begins March 23rd, following a shooting incident in 2025.
Fitzsimmons, accused of assault with a dangerous weapon, was shot by Officer Noonan during a restraining order confrontation.
Conflicting accounts and allegations of misconduct have surrounded the case; Fitzsimmons waived her right to a jury trial.

title: She’s running Boston for patients ‘rebuilding their lives step by step’
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Lina Sinisterra Mazariegos, 44, from Cali, Colombia, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
She views running as a means to understand herself and shares these experiences through her podcast.
Every mile of her marathon represents patients at Spaulding, supporting their journey towards independence and hope.

title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at the House of Blues, showcasing new and classic tracks with energetic showmanship.
Frontman Howlin' Pelle Almqvist engaged the audience with theatrical moves and witty banter about the band's journey.
Australian band The Chats opened with a garage-y punk performance, setting the stage for The Hives.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
McHugh, a cancer survivor, was diagnosed with Stage 3 breast cancer in 2023 but resumed running through therapy.
This is her third Boston Marathon and she is running to honor her late mentor and friend, Susan Hurley.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and others question Meta's plans for facial recognition in smart glasses, citing privacy threats.
Senators demand Meta disclose data handling practices and user consent options by April 6.
The letter precedes nationwide 'No Kings' protests scheduled for March 28 across Massachusetts, including Boston.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council postponed a vote on a $2.65 million plan for new firefighter gear free of PFAS chemicals.
Firefighters urge immediate funding as current gear contains PFAS, linked to cancer and other health risks.
Some councilors seek more review time, citing cost and durability concerns of the proposed PFAS-free gear.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro officials propose transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve due to costly repairs and maintenance.
Zoo faces $57 million in improvements and $900,000 annual upkeep amid city budget constraints and declining attendance.
Transition plan includes wildlife rehabilitation and native wildlife focus; operations remain unchanged until September 7.
